citations.quote: This email contains a copy of the Tenancy Agreement & Code of Conduct please have a thorough read through before we request you electronically sign the tenancy agreement if you decide to accept this offer of accommodation.
Based on previous residents' queries we would recommend paying particular attention to Section 16 "Access (Maintenance Work)", and Section 25 "Release from Tenancy" of the Tenancy Agreement.
1 attachment (3 MB) MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf;

This email contains a copy of the Tenancy Agreement & Code of Conduct please have a thorough read through before we request you electronically sign the tenancy agreement if you decide to accept this offer of accommodation.
1 attachment (3 MB) MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf;

Important Information: if after signing your legally binding tenancy agreement your situation changes e.g. you decide not to come to Edinburgh Napier University to study, you must contact Student Accommodation urgently and directly to discuss your situation and financial liability

findings.text: The email from Edinburgh Napier University to Anfal Elbushra (12/03/2026) encloses a tenancy agreement and code of conduct as an attachment named 'MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf'.
The attachment is approximately 3 MB in size and is a PDF file named 'MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf'.
The email references specific sections of the tenancy agreement: Section 16 (Access/Maintenance Work) and Section 25 (Release from Tenancy).
The email states the tenancy agreement is legally binding and advises contacting Student Accommodation if circumstances change.

proposed_artifacts.content:
## Extraction Gap Report: NAP-SRC-0028 / NAP-SRC-0029

**Task:** T-B02-0001 - Extract full tenancy agreement from attachment

**Status:** Partial extraction only. The email body was extracted, but the embedded PDF attachment 'MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf' (3 MB) was not processed.

**Evidence:**
- Both source PDFs yielded identical extracted text (SHA-256: 2dd6775d...)
- The extracted text is the email body only, referencing the attachment but not containing its content
- The attachment is a student licence/tenancy agreement PDF

**Recommendation:**
1. Re-extract the source PDFs using a tool that can extract embedded attachments (e.g., pdfattach, or a PDF library that supports attachment extraction)
2. Alternatively, obtain the original email with the attachment from the email system
3. Once extracted, OCR or text-extract the attachment to obtain the full tenancy agreement terms
proposed_artifacts.title: Extraction Gap Report for NAP-SRC-0028/0029 Attachment

proposed_tasks.instructions: Re-extract the PDF attachment 'MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf' from source NAP-SRC-0028 (or NAP-SRC-0029) using a tool that supports embedded attachment extraction (e.g., pdfdetach, or a Python script using PyPDF2/pdfplumber). Extract the attachment content as a separate PDF, then run text extraction/OCR on that PDF to obtain the full tenancy agreement terms. Output the extracted text as a new source artifact.
proposed_tasks.title: Extract embedded attachment from NAP-SRC-0028 PDF

risk_flags.text: The tenancy agreement attachment was not extracted. Key terms (e.g., rent amount, payment schedule, arrears provisions) are unavailable for analysis.
Without the full tenancy agreement, the legal basis for any claimed arrears cannot be verified from these sources alone.
The email references a 'legally binding tenancy agreement' but the actual signed agreement is not present in the extracted text.

summary: Extracted text from NAP-SRC-0028 and NAP-SRC-0029 shows the same email body referencing a tenancy agreement attachment (MISS ANFAL ELBUSHRA Student Licence.pdf) but the actual attachment content was not extracted. The extracted text is identical for both sources, indicating the attachment was not processed. The full tenancy agreement terms (Sections 16, 25, etc.) are referenced but not present in the extracted text. A follow-up task is needed to extract the attachment.

uncertainties.text: The extracted text from NAP-SRC-0028 and NAP-SRC-0029 is identical, suggesting the PDF attachment was not extracted. The actual tenancy agreement terms are not present in the extracted text.
The extraction method (pdftotext) may not have processed the embedded attachment. The attachment content (the tenancy agreement) is missing from the extracted text.
The SHA-256 hashes of the extracted text for both sources are identical (2dd6775d...), confirming the same email body was extracted from both PDFs without the attachment.
